search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae allergic rhinitis telehealth Issue 2 history red flags referral
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic allergic-rhinitis diagnosis or control score
automatic antihistamine, steroid, decongestant, immunotherapy, allergy testing, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Diagnosis and control require clinician assessment and appropriate context.
No medicine, dose, test, procedure, treatment, or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with clinician-established or suspected allergic rhinitis; severe asthma, pregnancy, occupational disease, structural obstruction, and systemic symptoms require separate pathways.
setting_applicability: UAE licensed telehealth, primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, or ENT follow-up.
UAE_applicability: DHA Issue 2 is directly applicable to licensed UAE telehealth; BSACI examination concepts require local adaptation.
recency_verification: Current DHA Issue 2 and the official BSACI revised guideline exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: DHA Issue 2 remains the registered UAE version; BSACI 2017 remains listed with recency limitations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA allergic-rhinitis history section supports interval change, nasal and eye symptoms, impact, associated features, relevant negatives, triggers, and concerns in UAE telehealth without assigning control or diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact BSACI examination section supports clinician-performed general and ENT examination without auto-populating nasal findings.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA investigation section supports review of existing specialist or investigation information when available without ordering tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact UAE management and referral section supports clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions only when discussed; no antihistamine, steroid, immunotherapy, or referral is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA red-flag section was reviewed for workflow safety, but broad generic red-flag text was not treated as proof of any specific finding.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.

unresolved_source_gaps: Allergen, seasonality, symptom pattern, eye and asthma context, medicine use and response, examination, testing, diagnosis, control, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
